name: Whitney Dankworth
title: Data Science, Machine Learning Engineering, Agentic Workflows
welcome:
Welcome! I'm Whitney Dankworth — Data Scientist at Microsoft with 5+ years of experience scaling AI adoption, governance, and data-driven insights.

This portfolio is an interactive terminal. Type a command below to explore.

Type 'help' to see available commands.

bio.summary: Data science can be a catch-all for many tools. My experiences in the statistical, computer science, and business worlds give me the expertise to frame and then tell valuable stories, building the right solutions for the problem at hand. I strive for technical excellence as well as clear communication to all stakeholders — from engineers to financiers to executives. Every day is an opportunity for learning and growth and to inspire the same in others.
education: B.S. Business and Political Economy, Computer Science — NYU Stern School of Business

highlights: Responsible for forecasting and analysis of Windows ecosystem health and growth. Pioneering first use of Temporal Fusion Transformers (TFT) in Windows to forecast customer retention, enabling more accurate and actionable insights for executive decision-making

Pioneered agentic workflows for time-series feature engineering, reducing time spent on feature engineering by 90% and enabling data scientists to focus on higher-level problem framing and model development

Created next-gen primary semantic data model for Windows devices across the company, enabling RAG-based AI agents across all of Windows data assets, modernizing 10+ year old infrastructure and transforming Windows data governance
Forecast expected impact of executive decisions to monthly active devices and consumer lifetime value
Modeled expected post-update device health for the Windows 11 rollout using ensemble ML and pattern mining
Analysis and modeling of in-device campaign noise and impact on customer retention
Formulated methodology and delivered a POC for first-in-class ML modeling of Azure infrastructure health
COSINE (Core OS & Intelligent Edge) Data Science Team, Azure deployments
Built and maintained data pipelines to automate routine dashboarding
Manipulated 100MM+ rows of data using SQL for user taxonomy project
Classified 900+ advertisements for CTR, CPM, and revenue optimization
Used data analysis to assist advisors in rebalancing $50MM of discretionary client assets
Conducted investment research and advised financial advisors

projects.title: Raspberry PI Email Summary Agent with Local Processing
projects.description: OpenClaw was an enticing offering, but security and privacy concerns prompted me to explore a local-first approach. I built a Raspberry Pi-based email summary agent that processes data locally and sends me a prioritized digest a few times per day. This project allowed me to experiment with local AI agent capabilities and edge computing while ensuring my data remains private and secure.
projects.status: Public - try it out with the code on my GitHub!

projects.title: WheresMy Bus — Real-Time Transit from the Terminal
projects.description: I kept missing my bus home from work because I was too deep in AI CLI tools like Claude Code and Copilot CLI. Ironically, I used one to fix the problem. WheresMy Bus is a Go CLI that gives me real-time King County Metro arrivals without ever leaving the terminal. It auto-detects whether I'm at home or the office via WiFi and shows the right stop — so now I just type `wheresmybus` and never miss the bus again.

projects.title: Agentic Workflows for Time-Series Feature Engineering
projects.description: Allowing AI agents to assist data scientists in time-series feature engineering for traditional ML at massive scale. Reducing time spent on feature engineering by 90% and enabling data scientists to focus on higher-level problem framing and model development.

projects.title: Windows Semantic Data Model & AI Agents
projects.description: Ideated and executed creation of the next-gen primary semantic data model for Windows devices across the company. Led transformation and modernization of Windows data governance, enabling retrieval augmented generation (RAG) based AI agents across primary Windows data assets.

projects.title: ICU Post-Discharge Mortality Prediction
projects.description: Academic project predicting long-term mortality for ICU patients post-discharge. Applied data science and ML techniques to healthcare data as part of NYU Stern coursework in Data Science for Healthcare.
